understanding.goal_understanding: The objective is to solve each level by moving and/or switching between selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, within the per-level step budget, progressing through all levels until the engine reports WIN for the final level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each action moves the currently selected piece on a grid where each cell corresponds to 3-pixel increments in the provided [x, y] coordinates. MOVE_* attempts to shift the selected piece one cell in that direction; movement can be stopped by walls/boundaries or by other objects, while some adjacent objects (blocks) can be pushed if they are directly in the movement direction and space/interaction rules allow the push. Levels may include multiple selectable pieces; you change which one you control by CLICKing using the exact pixel/grid coordinates listed for that piece in the state (same coordinate system as player.position and objects). The movement.{direction}_blocked flags are only hints about adjacency and do not guarantee the move will fail.

history.summary: Level 1 attempt 2/2 failed after 64 turns. No attempts remaining.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The objective is to solve each level by moving and/or switching between selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, within the per-level step budget, progressing through all levels until the engine reports WIN for the final level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each action moves the currently selected piece on a grid where each cell corresponds to 3-pixel increments in the provided [x, y] coordinates. MOVE_* attempts to shift the selected piece one cell in that direction; movement can be stopped by walls/boundaries or by other objects, while some adjacent objects (blocks) can be pushed if they are directly in the movement direction and space/interaction rules allow the push. Levels may include multiple selectable pieces; you change which one you control by CLICKing using the exact pixel/grid coordinates listed for that piece in the state (same coordinate system as player.position and objects). The movement.{direction}_blocked flags are only hints about adjacency and do not guarantee the move will fail.
